{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /config/.claude/skills/sc-task-unified-protocol\n\n> **NOTE**: Classification has already been performed by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 4.5 Test Failure Escalation Protocol (TFEP)\n\n**CRITICAL**: When tests fail during the Verification Phase (Section 4), agents MUST follow this protocol. Ad-hoc fixes are PROHIBITED.\n\n#### TFEP Prohibition Rules (VIOLATION-level)\n\nThese rules apply to ALL compliance tiers that run tests (STRICT, STANDARD):\n\n1. **VIOLATION**: Agents MUST NOT fix any code in response to test failures without completing the TFEP workflow.\n2. **VIOLATION**: Agents MUST NOT modify test expectations to make failing tests pass without adversarial validation.\n3. **VIOLATION**: Ad-hoc patches derived from test output are PROHIBITED. The agent must not read a traceback and immediately edit code to resolve it.\n\n**Permitted Exceptions** (MAY fix directly without TFEP):\n- Single `ImportError`/`NameError` in test scaffolding the agent just wrote, affecting <=2 tests, where the error is in the test file itself (not in implementation code).\n- Lint/formatting failures (trivially fixable, unambiguous root cause).\n- Deprecation warnings (not failures).\n\n**Valid Adversarial Outcome**: \"Test expectations are wrong\" is a legitimate conclusion from adversarial debate. When the debate concludes that test expectations are outdated rather than the code being wrong, this is NOT grounds for the agent to directly edit the tests — it must be presented to the user for review.\n\n#### Test Baseline Snapshot (Pre-Implementation)\n\nBefore implementation begins, the agent MUST record a test baseline:\n\n1. Capture the list of all existing test files and test function names at task start (e.g., via `uv run pytest --collect-only -q` or directory listing).\n2. Store this baseline in memory for the duration of the task.\n3. On any test failure, classify each failing test as:\n   - **Pre-existing**: Test name appears in the baseline (existed before this task).\n   - **New**: Test name does NOT appear in the baseline (written by the agent during this task).\n4. This classification drives the MUST-escalate vs MAY-fix-directly decision below.\n\n#### Escalation Trigger Detection\n\n**MUST escalate (trigger TFEP):**\n- Any **pre-existing test** fails (tests in the baseline that were not modified by the agent). This is the primary trigger — regressions in existing tests indicate the implementation broke something.\n- **3 or more new tests** fail simultaneously (indicates a systemic issue, not a simple typo).\n- **Runtime exceptions in implementation code** (TypeError, AttributeError, KeyError, etc. in the code being tested, not in the test scaffolding itself).\n\n**Escalation gradient (within-TFEP, for future forensic integration):**\n- Repeated failure (same test cluster fails after fix attempt) → escalate from light to standard\n- Multi-file blast radius from recent changes → escalate\n- Low-confidence root cause from adversarial debate → escalate\n- Unresolved adversarial outcome (no winner/tie) → escalate\n- Second failed retest → escalate\n- Cross-domain or non-obvious regression → escalate\n\n#### TFEP Execution Flow\n\nWhen TFEP triggers, execute the following steps:\n\n**Step 1: Halt and freeze**\n1. **STOP** testing immediately.\n2. **FREEZE** implementation — no further code changes permitted.\n\n**Step 2: Construct failure context**\n3. Build a `failure_context` YAML package containing:\n   - `test_names`: list of failing test function names\n   - `test_files`: list of test files containing failures\n   - `error_output`: full traceback/error output\n   - `expected_behavior`: what was expected\n   - `actual_behavior`: what actually happened\n   - `changes_made`: files changed during this task with descriptions\n   - `task_description`: current task description\n   - `test_baseline`: the pre-implementation test baseline\n   - `escalation_count`: which TFEP trigger this is (1, 2, or 3)\n4. Write context to `{output_dir}/context.yaml`.\n\n**Step 3: Invoke forensic**\n5. Determine the forensic tier based on escalation count:\n   - 1st trigger → `--tier light --intent triage`\n   - 2nd trigger → `--tier standard`\n   - 3rd trigger → **FULL STOP**. Report to user. Do not attempt further fixes.\n6. Invoke: `/sc:forensic --tier {tier} --intent triage --caller task-unified --context {context_path} --output {output_dir} --depth quick`\n7. The forensic pipeline runs autonomously through all its phases and returns a structured return contract.\n\n**Step 4: Consume forensic results**\n8. Read the forensic return contract from `{output_dir}/return-contract.yaml`.\n9. Handle based on status:\n   - If `test_is_wrong == true`: Present to user for review. Do NOT auto-fix tests.\n   - If `status == \"success\"`: Proceed to Step 5 (tasklist insertion).\n   - If `status == \"partial\"` or `recommended_escalation != \"none\"`: Increment `escalation_count` and return to Step 3.\n   - If `status == \"failed\"`: Report to user, halt execution.\n\n**Step 5: Tasklist insertion**\n10. Read `tasklist_insertion_path` from the return contract.\n11. Insert the remediation block into the current tasklist:\n    - Add `## Failure Remediation Plan (Adjudicated)` heading.\n    - Insert remediation tasks BEFORE existing test/verification tasks.\n    - Preserve original tasklist structure — append, don't replace.\n\n**Step 6: Resume**\n12. Resume execution with `--compliance strict` starting from the inserted remediation tasks.\n13. After remediation tasks complete, re-run the original test suite.\n14. If tests pass: TFEP resolved successfully. Produce incident report and continue.\n15. If tests fail again: Increment `escalation_count`, return to Step 2.\n\n#### TFEP Incident Reporting\n\nAfter each TFEP resolution (success or escalation), produce `tfep-incident-report.md`:\n\n```markdown\n# TFEP Incident Report\n\n- **Trigger**: {which threshold rule fired}\n- **Escalation count**: {1, 2, or 3}\n- **Failing tests**: {test names and classification}\n- **Root cause**: {summary from rca-verdict.md}\n- **Solution**: {summary from solution-verdict.md}\n- **Outcome**: {success / escalated / failed}\n- **Forensic artifacts**: {path to output_dir}\n```\n\nThis report is committed to git alongside other forensic artifacts.\n\n#### Escalation Budget\n\n```\n1st TFEP trigger  → /sc:forensic --tier light --intent triage    (~5-8K tokens)\n2nd TFEP trigger  → /sc:forensic --tier standard                 (~15-20K tokens)\n3rd TFEP trigger  → FULL STOP. Report to user. Do not attempt further fixes.\n```\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- Circuit breakers: See MCP.md\n- Routing logic: See ORCHESTRATOR.md\n\n\nARGUMENTS: Execute all tasks in \"\"\"\n   121→    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n   122→    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n   123→\n   124→    for finding in findings:\n   125→        if not finding.files_affected:\n   126→ 220→) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n   221→    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n   222→\n   223→    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n   224→    Returns 257→\n   258→\n   259→def _handle_failure(\n   260→    failed_file: str,\n   261→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   262→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   263→    executor: procedure:\n   268→    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n   269→    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n   270→    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n   271→    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n   272→    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n   273→\n   274→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   275→    \"\"\"\n   276→    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n   277→    if executor is not None:\n   278→        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n   279→\n   280→    # Step 2: Rollback ALL target files\n   281→    restore_from_snapshots(all_target_files)\n   282→    _log.info(\"Rolled back all %d target files\", len(all_target_files))\n   283→\n   284→    # Steps 3-4: Mark findings as FAILED\n   285→    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n   286→    for file_path, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n   287→        for finding in file_findings:\n   288→            if finding.id not in {f.id for f in all_findings}:\n   289→                # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n   290→                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n   291→                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n   292→                all_findings.append(finding)\n   293→\n   294→    return all_findings\n   295→\n   296→\n   297→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   298→# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n   299→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   300→\n   301→\n   302→def _handle_success(\n   303→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   304→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   305→) -> list[Finding]:\n   306→    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n   307→\n   308→    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n   309→    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n   310→\n   311→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   312→    \"\"\"\n   313→    # Delete snapshots\n   314→    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n   315→\n   316→    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n   317→    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n   318→    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n   319→    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n   320→        for finding in file_findings:\n   321→            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n   322→                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n   323→                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n   324→                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n   325→                # SKIPPED findings stay SKIPPED\n   326→                all_findings.append(finding)\n   327→\n   328→    return all_findings\n   329→\n   330→\n   331→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   332→# T04.09 -- Tasklist Outcome Writer (Two-Write Model)\n   333→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   334→\n   335→\n   336→def update_remediation_tasklist(\n   337→    tasklist_path: str,\n   338→    findings: list[Finding],\n   339→) -> None:\n   340→    \"\"\"Update existing remediation-tasklist.md with per-finding outcomes.\n   341→\n   342→    This is the second write of the two-write model (first write in T03.04).\n   343→    Updates finding statuses and checkboxes, writes back atomically.\n   344→\n   345→    Uses atomic write (tmp + os.replace) per NFR-005.\n   346→    \"\"\"\n   347→    path = Path(tasklist_path)\n   348→    content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   349→\n   350→    # Build status lookup\n   351→    status_map: dict[str, str] = {f.id: f.status for f in findings}\n   352→\n   353→    # Update frontmatter counts\n   354→    content = _update_frontmatter_counts(content, findings)\n   355→\n   356→    # Update finding entries\n   357→    content = _update_finding_entries(content, status_map)\n   358→\n   359→    # Atomic write\n   360→    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   361→    tmp.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   362→    os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n   363→\n   364→    _log.info(\"Updated remediation tasklist: %s\", tasklist_path)\n   365→\n   366→\n   367→def _update_frontmatter_counts(content: str, findings: list[Finding]) -> str:\n   368→    \"\"\"Update YAML frontmatter counts based on final statuses.\"\"\"\n   369→    actionable = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\", \"PENDING\"))\n   370→    skipped = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status == \"SKIPPED\")\n   371→\n   372→    content = re.sub(\n   373→        r\"^actionable: \\d+\",\n   374→        f\"actionable: {actionable}\",\n   375→        content,\n   376→        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n   377→    )\n   378→    content = re.sub(\n   379→        r\"^skipped: \\d+\",\n   380→        f\"skipped: {skipped}\",\n   381→        content,\n   382→        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n   383→    )\n   384→    return content\n   385→\n   386→\n   387→def _update_finding_entries(content: str, status_map: dict[str, str]) -> str:\n   388→    \"\"\"Update checklist entries with final statuses.\"\"\"\n   389→    for finding_id, status in status_map.items():\n   390→        if status == \"FIXED\":\n   391→            # Change - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS to - [x] F-XX | file | FIXED\n   392→            content = re.sub(\n   393→                rf\"^(- )\\[ \\]( {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n   394→                rf\"\\1[x]\\2 FIXED\\3\",\n   395→                content,\n   396→                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n   397→            )\n   398→        elif status == \"FAILED\":\n   399→            # Keep - [ ] but update status to FAILED\n   400→            content = re.sub(\n   401→                rf\"^(- \\[ \\] {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n   402→                rf\"\\1 FAILED\\2\",\n   403→                content,\n   404→                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n   405→            )\n   406→        # SKIPPED entries already have [x] from T03.04, leave unchanged\n   407→\n   408→    return content\n   409→\n   410→\n   411→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   412→# T04.05 + T04.07 + T04.08 -- Main Execution Coordinator\n   413→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   414→\n   415→\n   416→def _check_diff_size(\n   417→    target_file: str,\n   418→    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n   419→) -> bool:\n   420→    \"\"\"Check whether the patch for a target file exceeds the diff-size threshold.\n   421→\n   422→    Compares the .pre-remediate snapshot against the current file content.\n   423→    Returns True if the diff is within threshold (or override is set), False otherwise.\n   424→    \"\"\"\n   425→    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n   426→    current = Path(target_file)\n   427→\n   428→    if not snapshot.exists() or not current.exists():\n   429→        return True  # Cannot check; allow to proceed\n   430→\n   431→    original_size = snapshot.stat().st_size\n   432→    if original_size == 0:\n   433→        return True  # Empty original; allow any change\n   434→\n   435→    original_content = snapshot.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   436→    current_content = current.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   437→\n   438→    # Calculate diff as percentage of changed lines\n   439→    original_lines = original_content.splitlines()\n   440→    current_lines = current_content.splitlines()\n   441→\n   442→    # Simple diff metric: lines that differ / max(original, current) lines\n   443→    max_lines = max(len(original_lines), len(current_lines))\n   444→    if max_lines == 0:\n   445→        return True\n   446→\n   447→    matching = sum(\n   448→        1 for a, b in zip(original_lines, current_lines) if a == b\n   449→    )\n   450→    changed_lines = max_lines - matching\n   451→    diff_pct = (changed_lines / max_lines) * 100\n   452→\n   453→    if diff_pct > _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT:\n   454→        if allow_regeneration:\n   455→            _log.warning(\n   456→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%) but\"\n   457→                \" --allow-regeneration is set; proceeding\",\n   458→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   459→                target_file,\n   460→                diff_pct,\n   461→            )\n   462→            return True\n   463→        else:\n   464→            _log.error(\n   465→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%); rejecting.\"\n   466→                \" Use --allow-regeneration to override.\",\n   467→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   468→                target_file,\n   469→                diff_pct,\n   470→            )\n   471→            return False\n   472→\n   473→    return True\n   474→\n   475→\n   476→def execute_remediation(\n   477→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   478→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   479→    output_dir: Path,\n   480→    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n   481→) -> tuple[str, list[Finding]]:\n   482→    \"\"\"Execute remediation agents in parallel across file groups.\n   483→\n   484→    Orchestrates:\n   485→    1. Create snapshots (T04.03)\n   486→    2. Spawn parallel agents (T04.05) with timeout/retry (T04.06)\n   487→    3. On any failure: full rollback (T04.07), return (\"FAIL\", findings)\n   488→    4. On full success: cleanup snapshots (T04.08), return (\"PASS\", findings)\n   489→\n   490→    Returns (status, findings) where status is \"PASS\" or \"FAIL\".\n   491→    \"\"\"\n   492→    all_target_files = list(findings_by_file.keys())\n   493→\n   494→    # Step 1: Create snapshots\n   495→    create_snapshots(all_target_files)\n   496→\n   497→    # Step 2: Spawn parallel agents\n   498→    results: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] = {}  # file -> (exit_code, attempts)\n   499→\n   500→    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(all_target_files)) as executor:\n   501→        futures = {}\n   502→        for target_file, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n   503→            future = executor.submit(\n   504→                _run_agent_with_retry,\n   505→                target_file,\n   506→                file_findings,\n   507→                config,\n   508→                output_dir,\n   509→                all_target_files,\n   510→            )\n   511→            futures[future] = target_file\n   512→\n   513→        for future in as_completed(futures):\n   514→            target_file = futures[future]\n   515→            try:\n   516→                file_path, exit_code, attempts = future.result()\n   517→                results[file_path] = (exit_code, attempts)\n   518→\n   519→                if exit_code != 0:\n   520→                    # Step 3: Failure -- rollback ALL files\n   521→                    _log.error(\n   522→                        \"Agent for %s failed after %d attempt(s), triggering rollback\",\n   523→                        file_path,\n   524→                        attempts,\n   525→                    )\n   526→                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n   527→                        file_path,\n   528→                        all_target_files,\n   529→                        findings_by_file,\n   530→                        executor,\n   531→                    )\n   532→                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n   533→\n   534→                # FR-9: Diff-size guard -- reject patches that change too much\n   535→                if not _check_diff_size(file_path, allow_regeneration):\n   536→                    _log.error(\n   537→                        \"Diff-size threshold exceeded for %s, triggering rollback\",\n   538→                        file_path,\n   539→                    )\n   540→                    # Mark findings for this file as FAILED\n   541→                    for f in findings_by_file.get(file_path, []):\n   542→                        f.status = \"FAILED\"\n   543→                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n   544→                        file_path,\n   545→                        all_target_files,\n   546→                        findings_by_file,\n   547→                        executor,\n   548→                    )\n   549→                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n   550→\n   551→            except Exception as exc:\n   552→                _log.error(\"Agent for %s raised exception: %s\", target_file, exc)\n   553→                failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n   554→                    target_file,\n   555→                    all_target_files,\n   556→                    findings_by_file,\n   557→                    executor,\n   558→                )\n   559→                return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n   560→\n   561→    # Step 4: All agents succeeded\n   562→    success_findings = _handle_success(all_target_files, findings_by_file)\n   563→    return \"PASS\", success_findings\n   564→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01J92HZnPxz5TxuYsE2V9PAi","session_id":"2394baa0-0306-4a52-9af5-f8516958e0e3","uuid":"c5ffe7ef-8398-4f47-80f5-36ad4b72d778","timestamp":"2026-03-21T00:01:50.238Z"}

Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n   529→    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n   530→    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n   531→    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n   532→    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n   533→    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n   534→    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry\n   535→\n   536→    Args:\n   537→        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.\n   538→        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.\n   539→        output_dir: Directory for debate output artifacts.\n   540→        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for ClaudeProcess creation.\n   541→\n   542→    Returns:\n   543→        Verdict string: \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", or \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\".\n   544→    \"\"\"\n   545→    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '') or ''\n   546→\n   547→    # Step 2: Execute both in parallel\n   568→    prosecutor_yaml: dict = {}\n   569→    defender_yaml: dict = {}\n   570→\n   571→    if claude_process_factory is not None:\n   572→        def _run_side(prompt: str) -> dict:\n   573→            try:\n   574→                process = claude_process_factory()\n   575→                response = process.run(prompt)\n   576→                parsed = yaml.safe_load(response)\n   577→                return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else {}\n   578→            except Exception:\n   579→                return {}\n   580→\n   581→        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:\n   582→            p_future = pool.submit(_run_side, prosecutor_prompt)\n   583→            d_future = pool.submit(_run_side, defender_prompt)\n   584→            prosecutor_yaml = p_future.result()\n   585→            defender_yaml = d_future.result()\n   586→\n   587→    # Step 3 & 4: Score both sides (defaults to 0 on parse failure)\n   588→    prosecutor_scores = score_argument(prosecutor_yaml)\n   589→    defender_scores = score_argument(defender_yaml)\n   590→\n   591→    #  \"\"\"Credit budget when structural HIGHs decrease between runs.\n    48→\n    49→    Returns 0 when curr_structural_highs >= prev_structural_highs.\n    50→    Uses ledger.reimbursement_rate for content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n   151→\n   152→    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n   153→    FR-4.2: truncation markers include section heading.\n   154→    \"\"\"\n   155→    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n   156→    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n   157→        return content\n   158→\n   159→    omitted = len(encoded) -   f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n   199→        )\n   200→\n   201→    # Truncation priority (FR-4.2): prior summary first, then structural, then spec/roadmap\n   202→    # Truncate prior findings summary (least critical — cut first)\n   203→    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n   204→        request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget, heading=\"Prior Findings Summary\",\n   205→    )\n   206→\n   207→    # Truncate structural findings context (secondary)\n   208→    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n   209→        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n   210→        for f in request.structural_findings\n   211→    )\n   212→    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n   213→        structural_text, structural_budget, heading=\"Structural Findings Context\",\n   214→    )\n   215→\n   216→    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections (last resort)\n   217→    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n   218→        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n   219→        for s in request.spec_sections\n   220→    )\n   221→    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n   222→        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n   223→        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n   224→    )\n   225→    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n   226→    combined = _truncate_to_budget(\n   227→        combined, spec_roadmap_budget, heading=\"Spec/Roadmap Sections\",\n   228→    )\n   229→\n   230→    prompt = (\n   231→        f\"{template}\"\n   232→        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n   233→        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n   234→        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n   235→        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n   236→        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n   237→        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n   238→    )\n   239→\n   240→    # Final enforcement assert\n   241→    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   242→    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n   243→        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n   244→    )\n   245→\n   246→    return prompt\n   247→\n   248→\n   249→def score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n   250→    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n   251→\n   252→    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n   253→    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n   254→    \"\"\"\n   255→    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n   256→    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n   257→    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n   258→\n   259→    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n   260→    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n   261→\n   262→    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n   263→    word_count = len(argument.split())\n   264→    if word_count >= 100:\n   265→        imp = 3\n   266→    elif word_count >= 50:\n   267→        imp = 2\n   268→    elif word_count >= 20:\n   269→        imp = 1\n   270→    else:\n   271→        imp = 0\n   272→\n   273→    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n   274→    if confidence >= 0.8:\n   275→        lc = 3\n   276→    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n   277→        lc = 2\n   278→    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n   279→        lc = 1\n   280→    else:\n   281→        lc = 0\n   282→\n   283→    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n   284→    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n   285→    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n   286→    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n   287→\n   288→    return RubricScores(\n   289→        evidence_quality=eq,\n   290→        impact_specificity=imp,\n   291→        logical_coherence=lc,\n   292→        concession_handling=ch,\n   293→    )\n   294→\n   295→\n   296→def judge_verdict(\n   297→    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n   298→    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n   299→    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n   300→) -> tuple[str, float]:\n   301→    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n   302→\n   303→    BF-6 resolution:\n   304→    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n   305→    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n   306→    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n   307→\n   308→    Returns (verdict, margin).\n   309→    \"\"\"\n   310→    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n   311→    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n   312→    margin = p_score - d_score\n   313→\n   314→    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n   315→        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n   316→    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n   317→        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n   318→    else:\n   319→        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n   320→        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n   321→\n   322→\n   323→# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n   324→\n   325→def wire_debate_verdict(\n   326→    registry: Any,  # EDITABLE_FILES are SKIPPED with WARNING.\n   116→\n   117→    Returns (allowed, rejected) tuple.\n   118→\n   119→    Pure function: no I/O, no subprocess, no side effects (NFR-004).\n   120→    \"\"\"\n   121→    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n   122→    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n   123→\n   124→    for finding in findings:\n   125→        if not finding.files_affected:\n   126→ EDITABLE_FILES\n   136→        ]\n   137→\n   138→        if non_allowed:\n   139→            _log.warning(\n   140→                \"Finding %s references non-allowlisted files: %s -- SKIPPING\",\n   141→                finding.id,\n   142→                \", \".join(non_allowed),\n   143→            )\n   144→            rejected.append(finding)\n   145→        else:\n   146→            allowed.append(finding)\n   147→\n   148→    return allowed, rejected\n   149→\n   150→\n   151→def _basename(path: str) -> str:\n   152→    \"\"\"Extract basename from a path, handling both absolute and relative paths.\"\"\"\n   153→    return Path(path).name\n   154→\n   155→\n   156→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   157→# T04.05 -- Parallel Agent Execution list[Finding],\n   164→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   165→    output_dir: Path,\n   166→) -> tuple[str, int]:\n   167→    \"\"\"Spawn a ClaudeProcess for a single file group.\n   168→\n   169→    Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.\n   170→    \"\"\"\n   171→    base_prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)\n   172→\n   173→    # Inline embedding: read target file content into the prompt.\n   174→    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector).\n   175→    try:\n   176→        file_content = Path(target_file).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   177→        file_block = f\"## Current File Content\\n\\n```\\n{file_content}\\n```\"\n   178→        composed = base_prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + file_block\n   179→        if    max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   203→        model=config.model,\n   204→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   205→        timeout_seconds=_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n   206→        output_format=\"text\",\n   207→    )\n   208→\n   209→    proc.start()\n   210→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   211→    return target_file, exit_code\n   212→\n   213→\n   214→def 220→) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n   221→    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n   222→\n   223→    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n   224→    Returns (target_file, exit_code, attempt_count).\n   225→    \"\"\"\n   226→    # Attempt 1\n   227→    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n   228→\n   229→    if exit_code == 0:\n   230→        return target, exit_code, 1\n   231→\n   232→    _log.warning(\n   233→        \"Agent for %s failed (exit %d), restoring snapshot and retrying\",\n   234→        target_file,\n   235→        exit_code,\n   236→    )\n   237→\n   238→    # Restore snapshot before retry\n   239→    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n   240→    if exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n   250→\n   251→    return target, exit_code, 2\n   252→\n   253→\n   254→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   255→# T04.07 -- Failure Handling with Full Rollback\n   256→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   257→\n   258→\n   259→def _handle_failure(\n   260→    failed_file: str,\n   261→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   262→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   263→    executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None,\n   264→) -> list[Finding]:\n   265→    \"\"\"Handle agent failure: rollback ALL files, mark findings FAILED.\n   266→\n   267→    Spec section 2.3.8 five-step procedure:\n   268→    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n   269→    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n   270→    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n   271→    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n   272→    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n   273→\n   274→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   275→    \"\"\"\n   276→    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n   277→    if executor is not None:\n   278→        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n   279→\n   280→    # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n   290→                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n   291→                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n   292→                all_findings.append(finding)\n   293→\n   294→    return all_findings\n   295→\n   296→\n   297→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   298→# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n   299→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   300→\n   301→\n   302→def _handle_success(\n   303→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   304→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   305→) -> list[Finding]:\n   306→    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n   307→\n   308→    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n   309→    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n   310→\n   311→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   312→    \"\"\"\n   313→    # Delete snapshots\n   314→    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n   315→\n   316→    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n   317→    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n   318→    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n   319→    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n   320→        for finding in file_findings:\n   321→            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n   322→                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n   323→                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n   324→                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n   325→                # Cannot check; allow to proceed\n   430→\n   431→    original_size = snapshot.stat().st_size\n   432→    if original_size == 0:\n   433→        return True  # Empty original; allow any change\n   434→\n   435→    original_content = snapshot.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   436→    current_content = current.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   437→\n   438→    # Calculate diff as percentage of changed lines\n   439→    original_lines = original_content.splitlines()\n   440→    current_lines = current_content.splitlines()\n   441→\n   442→    # Simple diff metric: lines that differ / max(original, current) lines\n   443→    max_lines = max(len(original_lines), len(current_lines))\n   444→    if max_lines == 0:\n   445→        return True\n   446→\n   447→    matching = sum(\n   448→        1 for a, b in zip(original_lines, current_lines) if a == b\n   449→    )\n   450→    changed_lines = max_lines - matching\n   451→    diff_pct = (changed_lines / max_lines) * 100\n   452→\n   453→    if diff_pct > _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT:\n   454→        if allow_regeneration:\n   455→            _log.warning(\n   456→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%) but\"\n   457→                \" --allow-regeneration is set; proceeding\",\n   458→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   459→                target_file,\n   460→                diff_pct,\n   461→            )\n   462→            return True\n   463→        else:\n   464→            _log.error(\n   465→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%); rejecting.\"\n   466→                \" Use --allow-regeneration to override.\",\n   467→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   468→                target_file,\n   469→                diff_pct,\n   470→            )\n   471→            return False\n   472→\n   473→    return True\n   474→\n   475→\n   476→def execute_remediation(\n   477→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   478→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   479→    output_dir: Path,\n   480→    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n   481→) -> tuple[str, list[Finding]]:\n   482→    \"\"\"Execute remediation agents in parallel across file groups.\n   483→\n   484→    Orchestrates:\n   485→    1. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.03 -- Pre-Remediate File Snapshots\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef create_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Create .pre-remediate snapshot copies for all target files.\n\n    Uses atomic copy pattern: read -> write to tmp -> os.replace() per NFR-005.\n    Returns list of snapshot paths for rollback reference.\n\n    Raises FileNotFoundError if a target file does not exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    snapshot_paths: list[str] = []\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        src = Path(file_path)\n        if not src.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Target file not found: {file_path}\")\n\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        tmp = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n\n        # Atomic copy: read -> tmp -> os.replace\n        content = src.read_bytes()\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n        snapshot_paths.append(str(snapshot))\n        _log.info(\"Snapshot created: %s -> %s\", file_path, snapshot)\n\n    return snapshot_paths\n\n\ndef restore_from_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Restore all target files from their .pre-remediate snapshots.\n\n    Uses os.replace() for atomicity per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            os.replace(str(snapshot), str(file_path))\n            _log.info(\"Restored: %s from snapshot\", file_path)\n        else:\n            _log.warning(\"Snapshot not found for %s, cannot restore\", file_path)\n\n\ndef cleanup_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Delete .pre-remediate snapshots after successful remediation.\"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            snapshot.unlink()\n            _log.info(\"Snapshot cleaned up: %s\", snapshot)\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.04 -- File Allowlist Enforcement\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef enforce_allowlist(\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> tuple[list[Finding], list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Enforce file allowlist on findings.\n\n    Findings with ALL files_affected in EDITABLE_FILES pass through.\n    Findings with ANY file outside EDITABLE_FILES are SKIPPED with WARNING.\n\n    Returns (allowed, rejected) tuple.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O, no subprocess, no side effects (NFR-004).\n    \"\"\"\n    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n\n    for finding in findings:\n        if not finding.files_affected:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s has no files_affected, SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n            continue\n\n        # Check if any file is outside the allowlist\n        non_allowed = [\n            f for f in finding.files_affected if _basename(f) not in EDITABLE_FILES\n        ]\n\n        if non_allowed:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s references non-allowlisted files: %s -- SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n                \", \".join(non_allowed),\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n        else:\n            allowed.append(finding)\n\n    return allowed, rejected\n\n\ndef _basename(path: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Extract basename from a path, handling both absolute and relative paths.\"\"\"\n    return Path(path).name\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.05 -- Parallel Agent Execution with ClaudeProcess\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _run_agent_for_file(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n) -> tuple[str, int]:\n    \"\"\"Spawn a ClaudeProcess for a single file group.\n\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.\n    \"\"\"\n    base_prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)\n\n    # Inline embedding: read target file content into the prompt.\n    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector).\n    try:\n        file_content = Path(target_file).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        file_block = f\"## Current File Content\\n\\n```\\n{file_content}\\n```\"\n        composed = base_prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + file_block\n        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"remediate_executor: composed prompt for '%s' exceeds %d bytes;\"\n                \" embedding inline anyway (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n                target_file,\n                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n            )\n        prompt = composed\n    except OSError as exc:\n        _log.warning(\n            \"remediate_executor: could not read '%s': %s; using base prompt\",\n            target_file,\n            exc,\n        )\n        prompt = base_prompt\n\n    output_file = output_dir / f\"remediate-{Path(target_file).stem}.md\"\n    error_file = output_file.with_suffix(\".err\")\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=output_file,\n        error_file=error_file,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    return target_file, exit_code\n\n\ndef _run_agent_with_retry(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n\n    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code, attempt_count).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Attempt 1\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n\n    if exit_code == 0:\n        return target, exit_code, 1\n\n    _log.warning(\n        \"Agent for %s failed (exit %d), restoring snapshot and retrying\",\n        target_file,\n        exit_code,\n    )\n\n    # Restore snapshot before retry\n    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n    if snapshot.exists():\n        os.replace(str(snapshot), str(target_file))\n        # Re-create snapshot for the retry\n        content = Path(target_file).read_bytes()\n        tmp = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n    # Attempt 2\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n\n    return target, exit_code, 2\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.07 -- Failure Handling with Full Rollback\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_failure(\n    failed_file: str,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n    executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None,\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle agent failure: rollback ALL files, mark findings FAILED.\n\n    Spec section 2.3.8 five-step procedure:\n    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n    if executor is not None:\n        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n\n    # Step 2: Rollback ALL target files\n    restore_from_snapshots(all_target_files)\n    _log.info(\"Rolled back all %d target files\", len(all_target_files))\n\n    # Steps 3-4: Mark findings as FAILED\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    for file_path, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in {f.id for f in all_findings}:\n                # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_success(\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n\n    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Delete snapshots\n    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n                # SKIPPED findings stay SKIPPED\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.09 -- Tasklist Outcome Writer (Two-Write Model)\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef update_remediation_tasklist(\n    tasklist_path: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Update existing remediation-tasklist.md with per-finding outcomes.\n\n    This is the second write of the two-write model (first write in T03.04).\n    Updates finding statuses and checkboxes, writes back atomically.\n\n    Uses atomic write (tmp + os.replace) per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    path = Path(tasklist_path)\n    content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    # Build status lookup\n    status_map: dict[str, str] = {f.id: f.status for f in findings}\n\n    # Update frontmatter counts\n    content = _update_frontmatter_counts(content, findings)\n\n    # Update finding entries\n    content = _update_finding_entries(content, status_map)\n\n    # Atomic write\n    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n\n    _log.info(\"Updated remediation tasklist: %s\", tasklist_path)\n\n\ndef _update_frontmatter_counts(content: str, findings: list[Finding]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update YAML frontmatter counts based on final statuses.\"\"\"\n    actionable = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\", \"PENDING\"))\n    skipped = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status == \"SKIPPED\")\n\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^actionable: \\d+\",\n        f\"actionable: {actionable}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^skipped: \\d+\",\n        f\"skipped: {skipped}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    return content\n\n\ndef _update_finding_entries(content: str, status_map: dict[str, str]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update checklist entries with final statuses.\"\"\"\n    for finding_id, status in status_map.items():\n        if status == \"FIXED\":\n            # Change - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS to - [x] F-XX | file | FIXED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- )\\[ \\]( {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1[x]\\2 FIXED\\3\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        elif status == \"FAILED\":\n            # Keep - [ ] but update status to FAILED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- \\[ \\] {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1 FAILED\\2\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        # SKIPPED entries already have [x] from T03.04, leave unchanged\n\n    return content\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.05 + T04.07 + T04.08 -- Main Execution Coordinator\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _check_diff_size(\n    target_file: str,\n    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check whether the patch for a target file exceeds the diff-size threshold.\n\n    Compares the .pre-remediate snapshot against the current file content.\n    Returns True if the diff is within threshold (or override is set), False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n    current = Path(target_file)\n\n    if not snapshot.exists() or not current.exists():\n        return True  # Cannot check; allow to proceed\n\n    original_size = snapshot.stat().st_size\n    if original_size == 0:\n        return True  # Empty original; allow any change\n\n    original_content = snapshot.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    current_content = current.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    # Calculate diff as percentage of changed lines\n    original_lines = original_content.splitlines()\n    current_lines = current_content.splitlines()\n\n    # Simple diff metric: lines that differ / max(original, current) lines\n    max_lines = max(len(original_lines), len(current_lines))\n    if max_lines == 0:\n        return True\n\n    matching = sum(\n        1 for a, b in zip(original_lines, current_lines) if a == b\n    )\n    changed_lines = max_lines - matching\n    diff_pct = (changed_lines / max_lines) * 100\n\n    if diff_pct > _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT:\n        if allow_regeneration:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%) but\"\n                \" --allow-regeneration is set; proceeding\",\n                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n                target_file,\n                diff_pct,\n            )\n            return True\n        else:\n            _log.error(\n                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%); rejecting.\"\n                \" Use --allow-regeneration to override.\",\n                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n                target_file,\n                diff_pct,\n            )\n            return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef execute_remediation(\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n) -> tuple[str, list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Execute remediation agents in parallel across file groups.\n\n    Orchestrates:\n    1. Create snapshots (T04.03)\n    2. Spawn parallel agents (T04.05) with timeout/retry (T04.06)\n    3. On any failure: full rollback (T04.07), return (\"FAIL\", findings)\n    4. On full success: cleanup snapshots (T04.08), return (\"PASS\", findings)\n\n    Returns (status, findings) where status is \"PASS\" or \"FAIL\".\n    \"\"\"\n    all_target_files = list(findings_by_file.keys())\n\n    # Step 1: Create snapshots\n    create_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Step 2: Spawn parallel agents\n    results: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] = {}  # file -> (exit_code, attempts)\n\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(all_target_files)) as executor:\n        futures = {}\n        for target_file, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n            future = executor.submit(\n                _run_agent_with_retry,\n                target_file,\n                file_findings,\n                config,\n                output_dir,\n                all_target_files,\n            )\n            futures[future] = target_file\n\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\n            target_file = futures[future]\n            try:\n                file_path, exit_code, attempts = future.result()\n                results[file_path] = (exit_code, attempts)\n\n                if exit_code != 0:\n                    # Step 3: Failure -- rollback ALL files\n                    _log.error(\n                        \"Agent for %s failed after %d attempt(s), triggering rollback\",\n                        file_path,\n                        attempts,\n                    )\n                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                        file_path,\n                        all_target_files,\n                        findings_by_file,\n                        executor,\n                    )\n                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n                # FR-9: Diff-size guard -- reject patches that change too much\n                if not _check_diff_size(file_path, allow_regeneration):\n                    _log.error(\n                        \"Diff-size threshold exceeded for %s, triggering rollback\",\n                        file_path,\n                    )\n                    # Mark findings for this file as FAILED\n                    for f in findings_by_file.get(file_path, []):\n                        f.status = \"FAILED\"\n                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                        file_path,\n                        all_target_files,\n                        findings_by_file,\n                        executor,\n                    )\n                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n            except Exception as exc:\n                _log.error(\"Agent for %s raised exception: %s\", target_file, exc)\n                failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                    target_file,\n                    all_target_files,\n                    findings_by_file,\n                    executor,\n                )\n                return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n    # Step 4: All agents succeeded\n    success_findings = _handle_success(all_target_files, findings_by_file)\n    return \"PASS\", success_findings\n","numLines":564,"startLine":1,"totalLines":564}}}

58→\n    59→    Raises FileNotFoundError if a target file does not exist.\n    60→    \"\"\"\n    61→    snapshot_paths: list[str] = []\n    62→    for file_path in target_files:\n    63→        src = Path(file_path)\n    64→        if not src.exists():\n    65→            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Target file not found: {file_path}\")\n    66→\n    67→        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n    68→        tmp = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n    69→\n    70→        # Atomic copy: read -> tmp -> os.replace\n    71→        content = src.read_bytes()\n    72→        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n    73→        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n    74→\n    75→        snapshot_paths.append(str(snapshot))\n    76→        _log.info(\"Snapshot created: %s -> %s\", file_path, snapshot)\n    77→\n    78→    return snapshot_paths\n    79→\n    80→\n    81→def restore_from_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    82→    \"\"\"Restore all target files from their .pre-remediate snapshots.\n    83→\n    84→    Uses os.replace() for atomicity per NFR-005.\n    85→    \"\"\"\n    86→    for file_path in target_files:\n    87→        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n    88→        if snapshot.exists():\n    89→            os.replace(str(snapshot), str(file_path))\n    90→            _log.info(\"Restored: %s from snapshot\", file_path)\n    91→        else:\n    92→            _log.warning(\"Snapshot not found for %s, cannot restore\", file_path)\n    93→\n    94→\n    95→def cleanup_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    96→    \"\"\"Delete .pre-remediate snapshots after successful remediation.\"\"\"\n    97→    for file_path in target_files:\n    98→        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n    99→        if snapshot.exists():\n   100→            snapshot.unlink()\n   101→            _log.info(\"Snapshot cleaned up: %s\", snapshot)\n   102→\n   103→\n   104→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   105→# T04.04 -- File Allowlist Enforcement\n   106→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   107→\n   108→\n   109→def enforce_allowlist(\n   110→    findings: list[Finding],\n   111→) -> tuple[list[Finding], list[Finding]]:\n   112→    \"\"\"Enforce file allowlist on findings.\n   113→\n   114→    Findings with ALL files_affected in EDITABLE_FILES pass through.\n   115→    Findings with ANY file outside EDITABLE_FILES are SKIPPED with WARNING.\n   116→\n   117→    Returns (allowed, rejected) tuple.\n   118→\n   119→    Pure function: no I/O, no subprocess, no side effects (NFR-004).\n   120→    \"\"\"\n   121→    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n   122→    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n   123→\n   124→    for finding in findings:\n   125→        if not finding.files_affected:\n   126→            _log.warning(\n   127→                \"Finding %s has no files_affected, SKIPPING\",\n   128→                finding.id,\n   129→            )\n   130→            rejected.append(finding)\n   131→            continue\n   132→\n   133→        # Check if any file is outside the allowlist\n   134→        non_allowed = [\n   135→            f for f in finding.files_affected if _basename(f) not in EDITABLE_FILES\n   136→        ]\n   137→\n   138→        if non_allowed:\n   139→            _log.warning(\n   140→                \"Finding %s references non-allowlisted files: %s -- SKIPPING\",\n   141→                finding.id,\n   142→                \", \".join(non_allowed),\n   143→            )\n   144→            rejected.append(finding)\n   145→        else:\n   146→            allowed.append(finding)\n   147→\n   148→    return allowed, rejected\n   149→\n   150→\n   151→def _basename(path: str) -> str:\n   152→    \"\"\"Extract basename from a path, handling both absolute and relative paths.\"\"\"\n   153→    return Path(path).name\n   154→\n   155→\n   156→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   157→# T04.05 -- Parallel Agent Execution with ClaudeProcess\n   158→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   159→\n   160→\n   161→def _run_agent_for_file(\n   162→    target_file: str,\n   163→    findings: list[Finding],\n   164→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   165→    output_dir: Path,\n   166→) -> tuple[str, int]:\n   167→    \"\"\"Spawn a ClaudeProcess for a single file group.\n   168→\n   169→    Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.\n   170→    \"\"\"\n   171→    base_prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)\n   172→\n   173→    # Inline embedding: read target file content into the prompt.\n   174→    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector).\n   175→    try:\n   176→        file_content = Path(target_file).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   177→        file_block = f\"## Current File Content\\n\\n```\\n{file_content}\\n```\"\n   178→        composed = base_prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + file_block\n   179→        if  \" embedding inline anyway (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n   183→                target_file,\n   184→                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n   185→            )\n   186→        prompt = composed\n   187→    except OSError as exc:\n   188→        _log.warning(\n   189→            \"remediate_executor: could not read '%s': %s; using base prompt\",\n   190→            target_file,\n   191→            exc,\n   192→        )\n   193→        prompt = base_prompt\n   194→\n   195→    output_file = output_dir / f\"remediate-{Path(target_file).stem}.md\"\n   196→    error_file =    max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   203→        model=config.model,\n   204→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   205→        timeout_seconds=_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n   206→        output_format=\"text\",\n   207→    )\n   208→\n   209→    proc.start()\n   210→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   211→    return target_file, exit_code\n   212→\n   213→\n   214→def _run_agent_with_retry(\n   215→    target_file: str,\n   216→    findings: list[Finding],\n   217→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   218→    output_dir: Path,\n   219→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   220→) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n   221→    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n   222→\n   223→    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n   224→    Returns (target_file, exit_code, attempt_count).\n   225→    \"\"\"\n   226→    # Attempt 1\n   227→    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n   228→\n   229→    if exit_code == 0:\n   230→        return target, exit_code, 1\n   231→\n   232→    _log.warning(\n   233→        \"Agent for %s failed (exit %d), restoring snapshot and retrying\",\n   234→        target_file,\n   235→        exit_code,\n   236→    )\n   237→\n   238→    # Restore snapshot before retry\n   239→    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n   240→    if exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(target_file, findings, config, output_dir)\n   250→\n   251→    return target, exit_code, 2\n   252→\n   253→\n   254→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   255→# T04.07 -- Failure Handling with Full Rollback\n   256→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   257→\n   258→\n   259→def _handle_failure(\n   260→    failed_file: str,\n   261→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   262→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   263→    executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None,\n   264→) -> list[Finding]:\n   265→    \"\"\"Handle agent failure: rollback ALL files, mark findings FAILED.\n   266→\n   267→    Spec section 2.3.8 five-step procedure:\n   268→    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n   269→    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n   270→    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n   271→    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n   272→    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n   273→\n   274→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   275→    \"\"\"\n   276→    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n   277→    if executor is not None:\n   278→        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n   279→\n   280→    # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n   290→                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n   291→                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n   292→                all_findings.append(finding)\n   293→\n   294→    return all_findings\n   295→\n   296→\n   297→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   298→# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n   299→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   300→\n   301→\n   302→def _handle_success(\n   303→    all_target_files: list[str],\n   304→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   305→) -> list[Finding]:\n   306→    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n   307→\n   308→    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n   309→    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n   310→\n   311→    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n   312→    \"\"\"\n   313→    # Delete snapshots\n   314→    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n   315→\n   316→    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n   317→    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n   318→    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n   319→    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n   320→        for finding in file_findings:\n   321→            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n   322→                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n   323→                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n   324→                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n   325→                # Cannot check; allow to proceed\n   430→\n   431→    original_size = snapshot.stat().st_size\n   432→    if original_size == 0:\n   433→        return True  # Empty original; allow any change\n   434→\n   435→    original_content = snapshot.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   436→    current_content = current.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   437→\n   438→    # Calculate diff as percentage of changed lines\n   439→    original_lines = original_content.splitlines()\n   440→    current_lines = current_content.splitlines()\n   441→\n   442→    # Simple diff metric: lines that differ / max(original, current) lines\n   443→    max_lines = max(len(original_lines), len(current_lines))\n   444→    if max_lines == 0:\n   445→        return True\n   446→\n   447→    matching = sum(\n   448→        1 for a, b in zip(original_lines, current_lines) if a == b\n   449→    )\n   450→    changed_lines = max_lines - matching\n   451→    diff_pct = (changed_lines / max_lines) * 100\n   452→\n   453→    if diff_pct > _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT:\n   454→        if allow_regeneration:\n   455→            _log.warning(\n   456→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%) but\"\n   457→                \" --allow-regeneration is set; proceeding\",\n   458→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   459→                target_file,\n   460→                diff_pct,\n   461→            )\n   462→            return True\n   463→        else:\n   464→            _log.error(\n   465→                \"Patch exceeds %d%% threshold for %s (%.1f%%); rejecting.\"\n   466→                \" Use --allow-regeneration to override.\",\n   467→                _DIFF_SIZE_THRESHOLD_PCT,\n   468→                target_file,\n   469→                diff_pct,\n   470→            )\n   471→            return False\n   472→\n   473→    return True\n   474→\n   475→\n   476→def execute_remediation(\n   477→    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n   478→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   479→    output_dir: Path,\n   480→    allow_regeneration: bool = False,\n   481→) -> tuple[str, list[Finding]]:\n   482→    \"\"\"Execute remediation agents in parallel across file groups.\n   483→\n   484→    Orchestrates:\n   485→    1.          findings_by_file,\n   547→                        executor,\n   548→                    )\n   549→                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n   550→\n   551→            except
